task_completion.description: Create multiple simulated device adapters in OpenICE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenICE Supervisor UI showing multiple simulated devices (e.g., Multiparameter Monitor, Capnograph, Pulse Oximeter, Infusion Pump) added to the system, usually visible in a list or grid of active devices.

task_completion.description: Open the detail view for devices to inspect live data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenICE UI showing a detailed view of a specific device, displaying live data such as waveform graphs or large numeric readouts.

task_completion.description: Create the device parameter catalog file at the specified desktop location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with the file path '/home/ga/Desktop/device_parameter_catalog.txt' visible in the title bar or save dialog.

task_completion.description: List device types and observed parameters, classifying them as Numeric or Waveform.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains exact 'Device Type' names and lists of parameters under each, with explicit labels or categories indicating if they are 'Numeric' or 'Waveform'.

task_completion.description: Record the units and observed value ranges for the parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file includes units (e.g., bpm, mmHg, %, ml/hr) and numeric ranges (e.g., 60-100, 120/80) next to the listed parameters.

task_completion.description: Include a Summary section counting total devices and parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Summary' heading at the end of the text file containing numeric counts for the total number of devices found and the total number of parameters found.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to spawn devices and view data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenICE graphical interface being actively used, not just terminal commands or a hallucinated text file.

integrity.description: The documented parameters, units, and ranges appear to come from actual observation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file contains specific medical parameters (e.g., SpO2, Heart Rate, CO2) and realistic ranges/units that match what would be seen in the OpenICE UI, rather than generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: Agent used a text editor or standard file creation methods rather than relying on a pre-existing answer key.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or similar) is visible being typed into, or terminal commands show the file being constructed step-by-step.
